2604.25737.title: SAFEdit: Does Multi-Agent Decomposition Resolve the Reliability Challenges of Instructed Code Editing?
2604.25737.paper_summary: Instructed code editing is a significant challenge for large language models (LLMs). On the EditBench benchmark, 39 of 40 evaluated models obtain a task success rate (TSR) below 60 percent, highlighting a gap between general code generation and the ability to perform instruction-driven editing under executable test constraints. To address this, we propose SAFEdit, a multi-agent framework for instructed code editing that decomposes the editing process into specialized roles to improve reliability and reduce unintended code changes. A Planner Agent produces an explicit, visibility-aware edit plan, an Editor Agent applies minimal, literal code modifications, and a Verifier Agent executes real test runs. When tests fail, SAFEdit uses a Failure Abstraction Layer (FAL) to transform raw test logs into structured diagnostic feedback, which is fed back to the Editor to support iterative refinement. We compare SAFEdit against both prior single-model results reported for EditBench and an implemented ReAct single-agent baseline under the same evaluation conditions. We used EditBench to evaluate SAFEdit on 445 code editing instances in five languages (English, Polish, Spanish, Chinese, and Russian) under varying spatial context variants. SAFEdit achieved 68.6 percent TSR, outperforming the single-model baseline by 3.8 percentage points and the ReAct single-agent baseline by 8.6 percentage points. The iterative refinement loop was found to contribute 17.4 percentage points to SAFEdit's overall success rate. SAFEdit's automated error analysis further indicates a reduction in instruction-level hallucinations compared to single-agent approaches, providing an additional framework component for interpreting failures beyond pass or fail outcomes.
2604.25737.paper_summary_zh: 指導式代碼編輯對於大型語言模型（LLMs）來說是一個重大挑戰。在 EditBench 基準測試中，40 個評估模型中有 39 個的任務成功率（TSR）低於 60%，突顯了通用代碼生成與在可執行測試約束下執行指令驅動編輯之間的差距。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 SAFEdit，一個多代理框架，用於指導式代碼編輯，將編輯過程分解為專門角色，以提高可靠性並減少意外的代碼變更。一個規劃代理生成明確的、可見性意識的編輯計畫，一個編輯代理應用最小的、字面上的代碼修改，而一個驗證代理執行實際的測試運行。當測試失敗時，SAFEdit 使用失敗抽象層（FAL）將原始測試日誌轉換為結構化的診斷反饋，這些反饋被回饋給編輯器以支持迭代改進。我們將 SAFEdit 與 EditBench 之前報告的單模型結果以及在相同評估條件下實施的 ReAct 單代理基準進行比較。我們使用 EditBench 評估 SAFEdit 在五種語言（英語、波蘭語、西班牙語、中文和俄語）中的 445 個代碼編輯實例，並在不同的空間上下文變體下進行評估。SAFEdit 的 TSR 達到 68.6%，比單模型基準高出 3.8 個百分點，並且比 ReAct 單代理基準高出 8.6 個百分點。迭代改進循環被發現對 SAFEdit 的整體成功率貢獻了 17.4 個百分點。SAFEdit 的自動錯誤分析進一步表明，相較於單代理方法，指令級的幻覺有所減少，為解釋失敗提供了超越通過或失敗結果的額外框架組件。
2604.25737.author: Noam Tarshish et.al.
2604.25737.authors: Noam Tarshish, Nofar Selouk, Daniel Hodisan, Bar Ezra Gafniel, Yuval Elovici, Asaf Shabtai, Eliya Nachmani
